title: Boerhavia raynalii
taxonomy: Boerhavia raynalii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. This family consists of approximately 29 genera and 250 species, including the Boerhavia genus. The Boerhavia genus is native to tropical and subtropical regions, with several species found in Africa and Asia. Boerhavia raynalii is a species native to tropical Africa. It is also known as the 'Devil's cotton' due to its cotton-like seed pods.

In traditional medicine, Boerhavia raynalii is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. intro: Boerhavia raynalii is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Nyctaginaceae family, commonly known as the four o'clock flower. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, with reported health benefits including the treatment of various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Boerhavia raynalii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Boerhavia raynalii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
